metadata.title: The Last Signal — S01E03: The Pattern Breaks
metadata.logline: Harlan decodes the final transmission and learns who has been answering him.
metadata.author: genlock

flux-dev.negative: blurry, low quality, bright colors, cheerful

scenes.narrative: The storm peaks as Harlan decodes the final transmission.

intent.purpose: Pay off two episodes of pattern-decoding with the answer.
intent.conflict: Harlan wants the signal to be noise; the signal insists on being him.

scenes.narrative: Twenty years earlier: the wreck Harlan never speaks of.

shots.title: The decode
shots.action: Harlan cross-references the logbook against the tape counter, pocket watch open beside the receiver.
shots.visual_focus: The watch face reflecting the VU needle.
shots.audio_description: A brass pocket watch lies open beside a reel-to-reel receiver; its ticking hand and the meter's needle fall into the same rhythm.

intent.beat: The watch and the signal tick in sync.
intent.emphasis: Mechanical rhythm — the audience should hear the match before Harlan sees it.

flux-dev.text: Split diopter macro: brass pocket watch face razor sharp in foreground, VU meter needle sharp in background, storm light through window, teal and orange grade, 35mm grain.

seedance-2.subject: Weathered white man in his early sixties, heavy build, salt-and-pepper beard, deep-set gray eyes, wind-burned skin, navy wool keeper's coat over a cable-knit sweater, brass pocket watch on a chain.
seedance-2.action: 0:00-0:03: Insert — the watch hand and VU needle tick into sync. 0:03-0:06: Slow push in as both freeze on the same beat.

start_frame.prompt: Split diopter macro: brass pocket watch face razor sharp in foreground...

shots.title: The answer
shots.action: Harlan and Eli face each other across the receiver as the decoded voice plays.

flux-dev.text: Two shot across a reel-to-reel receiver, storm light, slow orbit blur, oppressive shadow, dreamy haze.

shots.title: The wreck
shots.action: Young Harlan drags a rowboat ashore; the pocket watch glints in the wet sand beside a broken hull.
shots.audio_description: From above, a lone figure hauls a rowboat onto a gray beach littered with wreckage; something small glints gold in the sand.

flux-dev.text: Establishing bird's-eye of gray wreck-strewn beach, lone figure dragging rowboat, desaturated 16mm documentary grain, overcast flat light, tiny gold glint in sand.

proposals.summary: prop_watch is plot-critical and appears in all three shots — confirm it should also be planted in the S01E02 recap.
The decoded voice needs a credited character (THE ANSWERER) for casting and TTS.
proposals.rationale: Voice plays in sh_2 but no character owns it.

characters.name: Harlan Morse
characters.identity_lock: Weathered white man in his early sixties, heavy build, salt-and-pepper beard, deep-set gray eyes, wind-burned skin, navy wool keeper's coat over a cable-knit sweater, brass pocket watch on a chain.
distinguishing_marks: missing tip of right ring finger
characters.default_wardrobe: Navy wool keeper's coat over cable-knit sweater

wardrobe_variants.label: Storm gear
wardrobe_variants.description: Yellow oilskin over the keeper's coat, sou'wester hat

wardrobe_variants.label: 1978 deckhand
wardrobe_variants.description: Canvas work jacket, rolled sleeves, no beard

voice.casting: in the register of a foghorn remembering being a man

characters.bio: Keeper of the Point Merrow light for thirty-one years; sole survivor of the Elder Maid wreck.
characters.want: To keep the light running and be left alone.
characters.need: To admit the signal is his own guilt, encoded.
secrets: He shut the lamp off the night of the wreck.

state_overrides.injuries: fresh cut across left eyebrow (origin of the scar)

character-reference-sheet.character_description: Weathered white man in his early sixties, heavy build, salt-and-pepper beard, deep-set gray eyes, wind-burned skin, navy wool keeper's coat over a cable-knit sweater, brass pocket watch on a chain.
character-reference-sheet.style: photorealistic, life-like live action shot on a DSLR camera with 35mm film and muted color tones

characters.name: Eli Morse
characters.identity_lock: Lean white man in his mid-thirties, storm-gray parka over a fisherman's sweater, short dark beard, his father's gray eyes, a radio operator's headset worn around the neck.

relationships.description: Came back with proof, not forgiveness.

environments.name: Lighthouse Interior — Storm Night
environments.description: The lamp room and radio bench of Point Merrow light during a full gale.
environments.style_lock: Cramped octagonal lamp room, brass and salt-pitted iron, rain hammering black windows, single practical desk lamp and the rotating beam bleeding through the floor hatch, teal-orange storm grade, 35mm grain.
environments.geography: Radio bench against the north window; stair hatch center floor; the beam sweeps clockwise, crossing the bench every eight seconds; door to the gallery on the seaward east side.

tod_variants.lighting: practical desk lamp + rotating beam spill
tod_variants.description: Black windows, rain streaks, beam strobing the room every eight seconds.

tod_variants.lighting: cold blue window light, lamp still on
tod_variants.description: The storm's tail; gray-blue light flattens the room.

locations.name: Point Merrow Lighthouse
locations.description: A 19th-century cast-iron lighthouse on a cliff above Wreck Beach, Maine coast.
locations.notes: One physical place; env_lighthouse_storm is its storm-night interior dressing.

props.name: Brass pocket watch
props.description: Tarnished brass pocket watch, cracked crystal, engraved "E.M."
props.notes: The engraving is the S01 finale reveal — keep it unreadable until then.

music_cues.notes: Swells under the decode, ducks for the dialogue exchange, resolves over the flashback.

character_arcs.notes: The decode forces the words he never said.
Came for an admission; leaves with the tape.

story_analysis.pacing_notes: Hold sh_1 longer than comfortable; the sync reveal earns the stillness.
